verification_note: Model IDs and pricing verified 2026-05-15. /model-audit re-verifies monthly. Verify live before any cost-sensitive operation.

codex.invocation: local Codex CLI — `codex exec` (general) / `codex exec review` (code review)
codex.auth: ~/.codex/auth.json (Codex CLI login — NOT a Keychain item)
codex.underlying_model: gpt-5.5 (OpenAI GPT-5.5 family, accessed via Codex)
codex.pricing_note: the maintainered via the Codex CLI account, not the stack's API metering. Codex calls are not metered into cost_log.
codex.notes: v1.1.1 adaptation: the stack reaches the OpenAI / GPT-5.5 family through the locally-installed, authenticated Codex CLI rather than a direct OpenAI API key. Claude Code cannot run subagents on non-Claude models, so adversarial roles (reviewer, product-critic, security-auditor) run on a Claude model and orchestrate Codex via bash. Replaces the artifacts' `openai` provider block. See ADR-011.

gemini.invocation: local Gemini CLI — `gemini -p "<prompt>"` (non-interactive); run from repo root for whole-repo context
gemini.auth: ~/.gemini/ (Gemini CLI login — NOT a Keychain item)
gemini.underlying_model: gemini-2.5-pro family (Gemini, accessed via the Gemini CLI; CLI selects model, override with -m)
gemini.pricing_note: the maintainered via the Gemini CLI account, not the stack's API metering. Gemini calls are not metered into cost_log.
gemini.notes: v1.1.1 adaptation: the stack reaches the Gemini family through the locally-installed, authenticated Gemini CLI rather than a direct Google AI Studio API key. Claude Code cannot run subagents on non-Claude models, so Gemini roles (architecture-critic, red-team, historian) run on a Claude model and orchestrate the Gemini CLI via bash. Replaces the artifacts' API-key `google` provider block. See ADR-012.

reviewer.notes: v1.1.1: adversarial review via local Codex CLI — see ADR-011.

product-critic.notes: v1.1.1: product critique via local Codex CLI — see ADR-011.

red-team.notes: v1.1.1: red-team analysis via local Gemini CLI — see ADR-012.

security-auditor.notes: v1.1.1: primary audit via local Codex CLI; belt-and-suspenders Opus second pass on novel crypto/auth — see ADR-011.

architecture-critic.notes: v1.1.1: whole-repo architectural critique via local Gemini CLI — see ADR-012.

historian.notes: v1.1.1: long-context history review via local Gemini CLI — see ADR-012. Tier 4 subagent.

foreman-team-lead.notes: v1.1: Used in agent-teams orchestration mode only. Main-thread mode uses foreman as a skill — no subagent invocation.

local-ops.notes: Tier 5 local subagent. Model string must match an ollama tag that tier-installer.sh actually pulls and HARDWARE.md recommends — keep these three in sync.

notes: v1.1.1 change: OpenAI/GPT-5.5-family work routes through the local Codex CLI (provider `codex`); Gemini-family work routes through the local Gemini CLI (provider `gemini`). Neither uses a direct API key. Adversarial roles run on a Claude model and orchestrate the respective CLI. See ADR-011 (Codex) and ADR-012 (Gemini).
v1.1 change: Foreman is a skill (skills/foreman/SKILL.md) in main-thread orchestration mode. Only in agent-teams mode is foreman a separate session (team-lead role).
Pricing verified against vendor docs on 2026-05-15. Anthropic: platform.claude.com. Apr 2026 friction was a 3.4x cost underforecast — verify live before any cost-sensitive operation.
/model-audit skill (Tier 4) re-verifies monthly and proposes changes.

Codex and Gemini calls are billed via their own CLI accounts and are NOT metered into cost_log — Tier 4 cost reporting does not see adversarial-review / red-team / architecture-critic / historian spend.
Gemini 3.1 Pro (2M context, released Feb 2026) — the Gemini CLI selects its own model; revisit the -m override in the next /model-audit.
